label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, there are five fruits: apples, oranges, watermelons, pears, and mangoes. They are arranged in order of their prices, from most expensive to least expensive. The problem gives us some clues about their relative prices, and we have to determine which one is the most expensive based on those clues. First, let's list out the information given: 1. Apples are more expensive than oranges. So, A > O. 2. Oranges are more expensive than mangoes. So, O > M. 3. Apples are the third-most expensive. So, there are two fruits more expensive than apples. 4. Pears are more expensive than watermelons. So, P > W. Our goal is to figure out which of the options (A to E) is correct, i.e., which fruit is the most expensive. Let me start by trying to determine the order of the fruits from most expensive to least expensive based on the given information. From clue 3, we know that apples are the third-most expensive. So, there are two fruits that are more expensive than apples, and two fruits that are less expensive than apples. Since we have five fruits, and apples are third, that means the order would look like: 1. [Fruit 1] 2. [Fruit 2] 3. Apples 4. [Fruit 4] 5. [Fruit 5] Now we know from clue 1 that apples are more expensive than oranges, so oranges must be either 4th or 5th. But we also know from clue 2 that oranges are more expensive than mangoes, so mangoes must be the least expensive of the two. That means mangoes must be 5th, and oranges must be 4th. So now the order looks like: 1. [Fruit 1] 2. [Fruit 2] 3. Apples 4. Oranges 5. Mangoes Now we also know from clue 4 that pears are more expensive than watermelons. So, P > W. We have two fruits left to place: pears and watermelons. And we have two positions left: 1st and 2nd. Let's consider the possibilities: If pears are more expensive than watermelons, then either: - Pears are 1st and watermelons are 2nd, or - Pears are 2nd and watermelons are 3rd or below - Pears are 1st and watermelons are 3rd or below But we know from clue 3 that apples are 3rd, so watermelons can't be 3rd because apples are already there. So the only possible placements for pears and watermelons are: - Pears are 1st and watermelons are 2nd, or - Pears are 2nd and watermelons are 5th But we already have oranges at 4th and mangoes at 5th, so watermelons can't be 5th because mangoes are already there. So the only possibility is that pears are 1st and watermelons are 2nd. So the complete order from most expensive to least expensive is: 1. Pears 2. Watermelons 3. Apples 4. Oranges 5. Mangoes This satisfies all the given conditions: - Apples are more expensive than oranges (apples are 3rd, oranges are 4th) ✅ - Oranges are more expensive than mangoes (oranges are 4th, mangoes are 5th) ✅ - Apples are the third-most expensive (apples are 3rd) ✅ - Pears are more expensive than watermelons (pears are 1st, watermelons are 2nd) ✅ So the most expensive fruit is pears, which means option (D) is correct. Let me double check to make sure I didn't make any errors. - Apples are more expensive than oranges: Yes, apples are 3rd and oranges are 4th ✅ - Oranges are more expensive than mangoes: Yes, oranges are 4th and mangoes are 5th ✅ - Apples are the third-most expensive: Yes, apples are 3rd ✅ - Pears are more expensive than watermelons: Yes, pears are 1st and watermelons are 2nd
